{"step_by_step_thinking": "Staphylococcus albus is a coagulase-negative staphylococcus, which is typically a commensal organism and rarely causes endocarditis. Streptococcus faecalis is a member of the normal gut flora and can occasionally cause endocarditis, especially in immunocompromised individuals. Salmonella typhi is the causative agent of typhoid fever and is not typically associated with endocarditis. Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a common cause of endocarditis, particularly in intravenous drug users and those with underlying heart conditions. Therefore, the least likely to cause infective endocarditis is Staphylococcus albus.", "answer_choice": "A"}
